pi_items.source: Wikipedia coordinates for Great Pyramid of Giza (29.97916667) rounded to 4 decimals.
Wikipedia coordinates for Great Pyramid of Giza (31.13416667) rounded to 4 decimals.
Mathematical conversion of 29.9792 degrees to radians.
Mathematical conversion of 31.1342 degrees to radians.
Task description requests demonstration of night sky during the age of Egyptian pyramids (~2560 BCE).
Calculated Julian Date for June 21, 2560 BCE (astronomical year -2559) is approximately 786575, matching the approximate claim of 786000.
Explicitly defined in export_result.sh and task.json description.
Explicitly defined in export_result.sh and task.json description.

privileged_info_summary: The task accurately targets the Great Pyramid of Giza with real-world geographical coordinates (~29.98N, 31.13E) and correctly calculates their radian equivalents (0.5232 rad, 0.5434 rad). The task requires simulating the sky circa 2560 BCE (astronomical year -2559), which corresponds to a Julian Date of approximately 786000. It requires creating lecture notes at /home/ga/Desktop/precession_notes.txt and screenshots at /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium. The software operates on real astronomical catalogs and relies on no synthetic external data.
